SECTION_TITLE: Purpose and Benefits
CONTENT:
From an enterprise architecture perspective, the IT Product is the central modeling object across all architecture layers. It enables: • Functional development planning • Portfolio and release planning • Centralized documentation of: o Security o Compliance o Data protection o Governance requirements The IT Product is the primary reference object for ensuring: • Compliance with the Federal Data Protection Act (BDSG) • Fulfillment of corporate due dili
subject: IT_Product

source_text_snippet:

CONTENT:
• Consists of IT Products Each IT Product is an application, business service, or business platform, including its associated technologies. These are delivered to internal users (employees) and/or external users (customers).

• Enables Operational Value Streams (OVS) Digital Solutions are designed to support and enhance OVS by deliv

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Modeling Guidelines
CONTENT:
A Digital Solution must follow the naming convention defined by the ART that owns it. This ensures consistency and traceability across the portfolio. Each Digital Solution must include at least one assigned IT Product. These IT Products represent the technical building blocks that deliver business functionality and are developed and maintained by agile teams within the ART.

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Modeling Hierarchies with Parent/Child Relations in
CONTENT:
LeanIX In LeanIX, Parent/Child relations are used to model hierarchical dependencies between Fact Sheets. These relations help structure complex systems, portfolios, or organizational units by defining logical containment or ownership hierarchies.
subject: Parent_Child_Relation

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Purpose and Benefits of Business Process
CONTENT:
Modeling Strategic and Operational Insight Business process modeling is a key element of the business architecture. It helps identify: • Processes that are strategically or economically critical, • Processes that are under pressure to change due to transformation, regulation, or inefficiencies. This insight supports prioritization and investment decisions across business and IT.
subject: Business_Process_Modeling

subject: Business_Process

• Digital Solution → Organization: Organizational units are responsible for or consume the digital solution.

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Definition: IT Product
CONTENT:
At Porsche, an IT Product is classified as either an Application or a Platform. Regardless of category, each IT Product must have clearly defined ownership and accountability. SECTION_TITLE: Responsibility for IT Components
CONTENT:
The definition of who is responsible for an IT component continues to lead to heated discussions in the company. Especially due to the establishment of the so-called departmental IT (P51), we are in a dilemma here from an IT point of view. The person specified on a component is typically the person responsible for that component for the entire organization in terms of operations, maintenance, security, and compliance. for a Business Capability Purpose of Business Capabilities in EAM A business capability serves as a stable, strategic abstraction of what an organization must be able to do to deliver value and fulfill its mission. SECTION_TITLE: Zusätzliche Relationen
CONTENT:
In addition to the automatically existing assignments between IT product and process, which are synchronized from BIC, business capabilities and modeling initiatives can be assigned to a process. Manual assignment of IT products via LeanIX should be avoided and should be done exclusively in BIC! Further Information Detailed technical and functional information about the synchronization between LeanIX and BIC can be found in the chapter LeanIX System

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Introduction
CONTENT:
This page describes the modeling guidelines for IT Components in the context of enterprise architecture at Porsche. It is based on the general modeling guidelines of PAG and includes specific instructions for creating and maintaining IT Component in LeanIX. The creation and editing of an IT Component fact sheet follows the standard procedure for creating a general fact sheet as described in "How to create a fact sheet". subject: IT_Component

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Interface and Impact Modeling
CONTENT:
• Focus on identifying actual interfaces and points of impact.
• In most cases, assigning only the directly affected IT products is sufficient.
• If IT Product fact sheets are properly maintained, indirect impacts (e.g. on business capabilities, IT components, or processes) can be inferred via the linked IT products.

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Subtypes in LeanIX
CONTENT:
In LeanIX, business capabilities are divided into two subtypes: Structured Element • Used to organize and group business capabilities hierarchically. • Cannot be linked directly to other fact sheets. • Typically represents Level 1 or Level 2 capabilities. Business Capability • Used to describe actual functional abilities in detail.
Business Capability • Used to describe actual functional abilities in detail. • Can be linked directly to other fact sheets

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Characteristics of an IT Product
CONTENT:
• It bundles business functions in a logically and functionally bounded way to process data.
• It may include custom-developed elements such as application code, configuration data, macros, etc.
• It can be a third-party software product, used as SaaS, cloud-based, or on-premises.
• It may represent a bundle of technical components that together provide infrastructure or business platforms.
subject: IT_Product

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Definition: Digital Solution
CONTENT:
In alignment with the Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) and extended by Porsche-specific terminology, a Digital Solution is defined as follows: A Digital Solution is a cohesive construct that delivers business value by integrating multiple IT Products. These IT Products are modular, technically and logically bounded units that implement specific business functionalities. Together, they form the technical foundation of a Digital Solution. In additi
subject: Digital_Solution

CONTENT:
by clearly defining how initiatives, business structures, and technical components interrelate. It enables traceability from strategic initiatives down to the technical implementation, supporting impact analysis, capability mapping, and IT landscape planning.
subject: Initiative

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Interfaces to External Systems
CONTENT:
In enterprise architecture, it is common for IT Products to interact with external systems— for example, to exchange data with banks, government agencies, or third-party services. These systems are considered external if they: • Are not part of the Porsche network (including ToGe networks), • Are not operated by PAG, a subsidiary, or a group brand, • Are not part of the internal Porsche IT landscape.
